Title: Successful Token Generation with Empty Body
Description: Successful Token Generation with Empty Body
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a POST request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with Content-Type: application/json and an empty JSON body {}.
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 200 OK.
3. Verify the response body contains a non-empty string representing the JWT token.
4. Decode the JWT token (e.g., using a JWT library) and validate its structure (header, payload, signature).
5. Validate the presence and format of standard claims like "exp" (expiry time) and "iat" (issued at time) in the token's payload.
Expected Result: HTTP status 200 OK. A valid, well-formed JWT token is returned in the response body, containing expected claims like "exp" and "iat".

Title: Missing Request Body Handling
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a POST request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with Content-Type: application/json but *without* any request body.
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 400 Bad Request or 422 Unprocessable Entity.
3. Verify the response body contains a clear error message indicating a missing or invalid request body.
Expected Result: HTTP status 400 or 422. An informative error message indicating a missing request body.

Title: Malformed JSON Request Body Handling
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a POST request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with Content-Type: application/json and a malformed JSON body (e.g., `{ "key": "value", }`).
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 400 Bad Request.
3. Verify the response body contains a clear error message indicating a JSON parsing error.
Expected Result: HTTP status 400 Bad Request. An informative error message indicating a JSON parsing error.

Title: Invalid HTTP Method (GET) Rejection
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a GET request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token.
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 405 Method Not Allowed.
3. Verify the response body contains an error message indicating the method is not allowed.
4. Verify the "Allow" header in the response indicates "POST".
Expected Result: HTTP status 405 Method Not Allowed. An error message indicating the method is not supported. "Allow" header should be present and list "POST".

Title: Invalid Content-Type Header Rejection
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a POST request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with Content-Type: text/plain and an empty body.
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 415 Unsupported Media Type or 400 Bad Request.
3. Verify the response body contains an error message indicating an unsupported content type.
Expected Result: HTTP status 415 or 400. An error message indicating an unsupported media type.

Title: Unexpected Extra Fields in Request Body
Description: Unexpected Extra Fields in Request Body
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a POST request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with Content-Type: application/json and a body like `{ "unexpectedField": "someValue", "anotherField": 123 }`.
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 200 OK (if fields are ignored) or 400 Bad Request (if strict validation is applied).
3. If 200 OK, verify a valid JWT token is returned, implying the extra fields were ignored.
4. If 400 Bad Request, verify the error message indicates invalid or unexpected fields.
Expected Result: HTTP status 200 OK with a valid token (extra fields ignored) OR HTTP status 400 Bad Request with an error message about unexpected fields.

Title: Rate Limiting / Repeated Request Handling (DoS Attempt)
Description: Rate Limiting / Repeated Request Handling (DoS Attempt)
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a large number of concurrent or rapid POST requests (e.g., 1000 requests in 10 seconds) to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with valid empty JSON bodies.
2. Monitor the response times and status codes.
3. Verify that after a certain threshold, the API starts returning 429 Too Many Requests status codes.
4. Verify that the API remains responsive and does not crash or degrade significantly under load.
5. Check for "Retry-After" header in 429 responses.
Expected Result: The API should implement rate limiting, returning 429 Too Many Requests after a threshold, and remain stable and responsive. "Retry-After" header should be present in 429 responses.

Title: Unnecessary Authorization Header Handling
Precondition: API endpoint is accessible and operational.
Steps:
1. Send a POST request to https://msdat-api.fmohconnect.gov.ng/api/auth/frontend-token with Content-Type: application/json, an empty JSON body `{}`, and an Authorization header (e.g., `Bearer invalid_token`).
2. Verify the HTTP status code is 200 OK.
3. Verify a valid JWT token is returned, indicating the unnecessary header was ignored and did not interfere with token generation.
Expected Result: HTTP status 200 OK. A valid JWT token is returned, implying the Authorization header was ignored as it's not required for this endpoint.
